items.title: Papers: Liverpool knock back Inter's Jones offer
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Monday's newspapers...

items.title: Liverpool reject £21.7m Inter Milan offer for Jones
items.summary: Liverpool reject a verbal offer of 25m euros (£21.7m) from Inter Milan for midfielder Curtis Jones.

items.title: Rashford might still join Barca - Monday's gossip
items.summary: Marcus Rashford might still join Barcelona, Paris St-Germain join the race for Crysencio Summerville, Manchester United have no interest in selling Benjamin Sesko, plus more.

items.title: Arsenal ‘ready to accelerate Morgan Rogers transfer pursuit’ and using England stars to help convince him to join
items.summary: ARSENAL are ready to ramp up their pursuit of Morgan Rogers – with England stars Declan Rice, Bukayo Saka and Eberechi Eze all helping to sell the dream. items.title: Drab Belgium held to goalless draw as spirited Iran side keep World Cup hopes alive in Los Angeles
items.summary: THE sun is rapidly setting on Kevin De Bruyne’s Belgian golden generation after drawing 0-0 with Iran at Sofi Stadium. items.title: Ollie Watkins reveals ‘underrated’ England star, 46, transformed his game after using him as a ‘sounding board’
items.summary: OLLIE WATKINS has revealed how "underrated” Michael Owen helped him get nastier. Aston Villa man Watkins reached out to England great Owen who suggested he had been too nice. items.title: Women's World Matchplay field confirmed with Greaves, Ashton, Sherrock in contention
items.summary: The field and draw for the Women's World Matchplay have been confirmed with Lisa Ashton set to defend her title.

items.title: Noel Gallagher backs Sun’s campaign to make Wonderwall England’s official World Cup anthem after ‘magical’ singalong
items.summary: NOEL Gallagher has backed The Sun’s campaign to make Wonderwall England’s World Cup anthem — declaring the post-match fan singalong with players “a magical moment”. items.title: Woad suffers play-off heartbreak as Yamashita snatches LPGA Tour win
items.summary: England's Lottie Woad narrowly missed out on her second LPGA Tour victory of the season after Miyu Yamashita snatched a dramatic play-off victory at the Meijer LPGA Classic.

items.title: Tuchel not afraid to shout at players - Watkins
items.summary: Ollie Watkins says Thomas Tuchel is "not afraid" to shout at England players as the manager aims to maintain training standards at the World Cup.

items.title: How many red cards have there been at the World Cup 2026, and what is the tournament record?
items.summary: THIS year's World Cup has seen a lot of players sent off already - with the record of 28 in 2006 under threat! THREE red cards were given between South Africa and Mexico in the World Cup opener - and it is not the only game with multiple red cards. The players' discipline in the...

items.title: Spurs' Bergvall exploring options away from club
items.summary: Sweden's Lucas Bergvall is facing an uncertain future at Tottenham with the midfielder keen for a new challenge.

items.title: Forgotten Man Utd flop wipes out his own team-mate and leaves him writhing in agony before World Cup clash
items.summary: FORMER Manchester United striker Wout Weghorst almost injured a team-mate with a clumsy challenge during a World Cup training session. items.title: Iran go top of group with battling draw vs frustrated 10-man Belgium
items.summary: Iran battled to a well-deserved point against 10-man Belgium and moved top of World Cup Group G with a 0-0 draw in Los Angeles.

items.title: Does it matter if Scotland lose and still make history?
items.summary: Does the performance, even the result, matter as long as Scotland qualify for the group stage of the World Cup after Wednesday's game against Brazil? Tom English ponders.

items.title: Ten-man Belgium remain winless after draw with Iran
items.summary: Goalkeeper Alireza Beiranvand made a string of impressive saves to keep alive Iran's hopes of reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time, as they earned a hard-fought point against 10-man Belgium.

items.title: VAR rules out Iran's Weghorst-esque goal
items.summary: Mehdi Taremi scores a goal similar to Wout Weghorst's effort against Argentina in the 2022 World Cup but it's disallowed by by VAR for offside.

items.title: Proposals to keep 'crown jewel' sports free on streaming services
items.summary: The government is proposing new legislation to prevent streaming and catch-up rights to some of the world's biggest sporting events - including the World Cup - being put behind a paywall for UK viewers.

items.title: Zinedine Zidane’s son seeing psychologist to deal with World Cup pressure.. and reveals MUM is biggest critic
items.summary: LUCA ZIDANE has revealed he has been working with a psychologist to help him cope with the pressure of keeping goal for Algeria. items.title: ‘Most unhinged moment so far’ – World Cup star picks up referee’s watch and puts it on his arm
items.summary: PARAGUAY hero Matias Galarza picked up the referee's watch and strapped it on during Saturday's win over Turkey. items.summary: ENGLAND'S stars are settling into life in Kansas City in very different ways – from cowboy hats and country music to celebrity haircuts, barbecues and fiercely competitive board games.
